input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi palette that balances Japanese minimalism and Scandinavian comfort. Use low-profile furniture, warm woods, woven textures and soft ambient light. Prioritize open sightlines, decluttered surfaces and a small dedicated work corner. Keep scale human and kid-safe: rounded edges, low heights, and natural materials.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: Warm neutrals and soft earth tones (creamy off-white base, honey oak midtones, muted caramel and warm greys) support both a serene Japandi look and family-friendly wear. Accent with charcoal for contrast and natural greenery for liveliness.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft, warm ambient light with layered sources: diffuse daylight where available, warm dimmable overhead pendants in dining, low floor and table lamps in living and bedroom, and a warm task lamp at the desk. Keep color temperature ~2700–3000K for intimacy.
materials: linen and wool textiles (cushions, curtains, rugs)
matte black metal for small accents and lamp arms
natural woven rugs (jute or tatami-edge blends)
textures: soft looped wool rugs with low pile
furniture_language: rounded corners for child-safety and calm silhouette
slim, tapered legs and cantilevered elements
single-statement pendant in dining; sculptural tabletop centerpieces
styling_rules: Keep surfaces mostly clear — 1–3 curated objects per surface (vase, tray, lamp).
Use layered rugs to define low seating and tea-nook moments.
Balance negative space with singular sculptural pieces — no gallery walls.
Introduce plants in odd numbers and near windows or corners for scale.
Prioritize durable, washable textiles in family zones; avoid pure white upholstery.
avoid: high-gloss finishes, chrome or flashy metal
overfilling the floorplan with large, tall furniture
busy patterned fabrics and excessive wall art
fragile glass decor at child height

room_guidance.headline: Low-profile family living with a compact work corner
room_guidance.guidance: Anchor the Nest with a low, light-oak sofa on a textured wool rug oriented towards the patio openings. Place a compact, wall-aligned standing desk in a corner near the large glass door so it benefits from daylight but does not block circulation to the patio. Include a low coffee table, one lounge chair or pouf for flexible seating, and a small play basket stored by the sofa. Maintain clear pathway to patio door.
must_include: compact standing_desk (folding/leaning type) near patio glazing
one child-safe pouf and a woven toy basket
must_avoid: oversized sectional that blocks circulation
tall freestanding shelving between sofa and patio door
fragile decor on low coffee table

room_guidance.headline: Serene bedroom with adjacent crib and modest storage
room_guidance.guidance: Compose the Dream as a calm sleeping zone: low platform bed centered on the longest wall, narrow dresser or built-in closet for clothing, and the crib positioned to keep a clear path to the door and closet. Keep decor minimal: one low bedside table lamp, a soft wool rug, and a small plant near the window. Ensure crib placement meets safety (clear of direct draft from window).
must_include: low platform bed (queen) with warm-wood frame
crib (placed with 36" minimum clearance from main walking path)
soft bedside lamp and low-pile rug
must_avoid: placing crib under a directly drafty window
large, high dressers that block closet access
heavy decorative lighting over the crib

room_guidance.headline: Compact, uncluttered kitchen with open flow to dining
room_guidance.guidance: Stage the kitchen as efficient and tidy: minimal counter clutter, warm oak or matte-lacquer lower cabinets, open shelving for a few ceramics, and a small herb pot by the sink. Use under-cabinet task lighting to suggest usable workspaces. Maintain a clear visual connection to the Dine area.
must_include: stack of neutral ceramic bowls on open shelf
matte cabinet finishes in warm neutral
must_avoid: full countertop of appliances or utensils
large island that would crowd the dining nook

room_guidance.headline: Intimate Japandi dining nook with rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Given the small footprint, use a round dining table (36–42 in) to maximize circulation. Center a woven rattan pendant over the table, and stage with 3–4 linen-upholstered chairs. A single stoneware vase with a dried grass stem creates an elegant focal point. Keep the bay/window seating clear to emphasize natural light.
must_avoid: rectangular large dining table
excessive place settings or table clutter

room_guidance.headline: Covered outdoor extension with low-seat tea nook feel
room_guidance.guidance: Stage Relax as a sheltered, low-slung seating area connected visually to the Nest and Dine. Use a low teak or oak bench, floor cushions or poufs, and a natural-fiber rug. Include a lantern-style floor lamp or string lights for evening warmth. Keep plantings minimal and wind-safe.
must_include: low wooden bench or tea-table with floor cushions
one weather-resistant lantern or string light
must_avoid: large, heavy outdoor sofas that block openings
fragile decor exposed to elements

warnings: Dining footprint (≈7.8 x 8 ft) restricts table diameter; recommend round table ≤42 in and avoid sideboards.
Standing desk in living must not block egress to patio — keep desk against wall or windowlight-adjacent corner.
Bedroom crib placement should respect minimum clearances and not obstruct closet or door swing.

creative_spec.overall_mood: Calm, warm, minimal — family-ready Japandi with soft daylight and layered natural textures.
global_style_notes: Keep silhouettes low and horizontal to emphasize Japanese influence and visual calm.
Use warm oak as the primary wood tone; repeat in furniture legs and small millwork for cohesion.
Limit decorative color accents to charcoal and muted green from plants.
Prioritize child-safe finishes and fabrics — rounded edges, stain-friendly textiles.
Lighting should be layered and dimmable; avoid harsh overhead white light.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: no bright saturated colors, no chrome or mirrored finishes, no cluttered surfaces, no large high-back/overstuffed sofas, no visible baby paraphernalia clutter (except the single staged crib), no cinematic dramatic lighting (keep warm, realistic ambient light), no unrealistic floating furniture or impossible shadows

evals.message: High-quality Japandi patio still that meets the brief: low teak table/bench, floor cushions, jute-like rug, weather-resistant lantern, and potted greenery are present. Bright, soft daylight and realistic materials/scale. Minor note: no string lighting visible (lantern satisfies lighting requirement) and a dining chair is visible indoors but does not detract from the patio staging. Ready for image-to-video generation.

events.message: Good Japandi styling, accurate bedroom identity, realistic lighting and solid composition. Major issues: the required standing desk is not present as a standing-height workstation (desk is staged at seated height), and the crib placement appears to leave less than the specified 36" clearance along the main walking path—reposition crib or bed to guarantee required clearance. Fix those and re-render still.
All room stills are ready for review.
